title: CloudFront Cache Keys, Origin Requests, and Invalidation · Spiral Review
summary: A delayed handoff reconstructs cloudfront cache keys, origin requests, and invalidation from browser, Node, TypeScript, AWS SDK v3, S3, CloudFront, security, billing, and stakeholder evidence and challenges one compile-time misconception. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for cache identity and freshness contract after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, include only representation-changing path, query, header, cookie, encoding, and authorization dimensions in the cache key.
In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, use origin request policy for additional required viewer data without unnecessary cache fragmentation or trust escalation.
In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, combine origin Cache-Control, minimum, default, maximum, stale behavior, validators, and error caching with content-change and privacy requirements.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and TypeScript Cache Key Identity · changed case · Review Cache Key Identity
steps.instruction:
review case — A review TypeScript file-delivery team handles case 2522 during a AWS SDK v3 dependency canary. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to align typed cache identity, origin forwarding, TTL, and release freshness; retain keyboard and screen-reader progress, cancellation, error, and recovery evidence; then double concurrent Upload instances and preserve aggregate memory and worker budgets. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. Node and AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, PostgreSQL, credentials, host state, handle and stream checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

Which action demonstrates Cache Key Identity without dropping earlier contracts?

Competency probe: In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, configure Brotli or gzip variants, Accept-Encoding cache behavior, already-compressed media, content length, and changed client capability evidence.

For S3, CloudFront, and TypeScript Separate evidence from confidence · Review Compression Vary, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, file, object, policy, transfer, integrity, cache, fault, cost, restore, and controlled AWS transfer evidence: CloudFront compression reduces every file type and never changes cache identity. Type annotations, generated SDK shapes, and resolved promises do not strengthen that runtime claim.

stimulus.title: Compression Vary case record
stimulus.caption: Three excerpts from a learner evidence packet; only one connects a claim to a reproducible check.

lines.label: assumption
lines.text: A review TypeScript file-delivery team handles case 3129 during a regional recovery and budget exercise. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to align typed cache identity, origin forwarding, TTL, and release freshness; do not treat TypeScript types, a resolved promise, CORS, ETag, or an emulator as stronger proof than it is; then change region, retrieval, or transfer cost and defend the revised budget action. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. Node and AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, PostgreSQL, credentials, host state, handle and stream checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, configure Brotli or gzip variants, Accept-Encoding cache behavior, already-compressed media, content length, and changed client capability evidence.

lines.label: risk
lines.text: CloudFront compression reduces every file type and never changes cache identity. Type annotations, generated SDK shapes, and resolved promises do not strengthen that runtime claim.

lines.label: evidence
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, configure Brotli or gzip variants, Accept-Encoding cache behavior, already-compressed media, content length, and changed client capability evidence. Artifact under review: cache identity and freshness contract.

options.text: For separate evidence from confidence, apply Compression Vary without its stated preconditions, omit the cache identity and freshness contract trace and counterexample, and treat one plausible output as complete proof. This does not satisfy Compression Vary for cache identity and freshness contract.
The record names the assumption, applies Compression Vary, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.

The Compression Vary record says cache identity and freshness contract looks right but supplies no governing condition, observation, changed case, or counterexample. That claim fails the separate evidence from confidence evidence boundary for Compression Vary.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, configure Brotli or gzip variants, Accept-Encoding cache behavior, already-compressed media, content length, and changed client capability evidence.
